theme: What a CFO said 6 months after the trip — unscripted

linkedin.text:
We don't run testimonials at the end of trips. Everyone loves an event when they're at it.

We ask CFOs 6 months later. That's when the answer matters.

Here's what one said this March, about a trip we produced in October 2025:

"I'll be honest. When the CMO pitched it, I was the no vote.

€485k for 4 days of an offsite for 130 people — when we had a hiring freeze on. I asked her: tell me what changes if we don't do this.

She didn't have a number. So I approved it on her instinct and my reluctance.

Six months in, here's what I'm seeing:

The sales team that did the trip closed 38% more in Q4 than Q3. Not a coincidence — they came back with new internal alliances. Two engineers in our R&D team who'd been quietly interviewing elsewhere both withdrew their candidacies in November. That's roughly €380k of replacement cost we didn't have to spend.

And the thing I didn't expect — the WhatsApp group from the trip is still active. Five months in. Internal questions get answered in 11 minutes on average. Before, it was Slack threads dying after two responses.

I was wrong. Not because I miscalculated. Because I was measuring the wrong thing.

The ROI of these events isn't on the post-trip survey. It's in the operational metrics 90-180 days later. That's where the actual lift shows up.

Next year I'm pre-approving the budget in Q1. The discussion is just where."

— CFO, Israeli industrial tech company, 280 employees.

This is the kind of feedback we build for. Not the post-event high. The 6-month operational reality.

📩 alon@upe.co.il

Alon Ouaknine

#Testimonial #IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents #UproductionEvents #MICE #CFO #EventROI #B2B #LeadershipDevelopment #CorporateTravel
